summary: Official Windows service control for Print Device Configuration Service (PrintDeviceConfigurationService). A local SCM snapshot now confirms the exact service identifier, display name, binary path, and current start type on the review host. The app disables the same service by setting its start mode to Disabled. This record stays in review because the current pass did not re-capture a machine-checkable exact Microsoft guidance row from the live services page.

setting.name: Print Device Configuration Service
setting.casual_explanation: This service manages installation details for IPP and UP printers. Disabling it can stop that printer-installation support.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable it on systems that do not install printers or on minimal builds where printer setup features are not needed.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Disabling it can reduce unused printer-setup plumbing, but printer installations in progress may be canceled and related setup flows can stop working.
setting.professional_notes: A local SCM snapshot confirms the exact PrintDeviceConfigurationService service identity on the review host: display name `Print Device Configuration Service`, binary path `C:\Windows\System32\svchost.exe -k LocalSystemNetworkRestricted -p`, and current start type `3 DEMAND_START`. The app uses a service start-mode tweak that disables `PrintDeviceConfigurationService`. Earlier research summarized the Microsoft Windows IoT Enterprise services page as documenting this service with a Manual default and `OK to disable`, but this review pass did not re-capture a machine-checkable exact row from the live page. Under the service-proof methodology, local SCM identity proof is strong but not enough by itself to restore validated status.

allowed_values.label: Windows default
allowed_values.meaning: The service starts on demand with Microsoft's documented default mode.

allowed_values.label: Printer device configuration disabled
allowed_values.meaning: The service no longer starts until its start mode is restored.

app_current_implementation.notes: The app disables PrintDeviceConfigurationService by setting its start mode to Disabled.

windows_defaults.label: Microsoft documented default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows systems using the normal Print Device Configuration Service configuration

states.rationale: Microsoft lists PrintDeviceConfigurationService with a Manual default start mode.

windows_defaults.notes: This baseline preserves the documented printer device configuration behavior.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
avoid_for: Minimal builds that intentionally remove printer setup support

states.rationale: Keep the documented default service mode.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want printer installation flows to remain available.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You never install printers and want this service family trimmed.
tradeoffs: The service can still start when Windows needs it.

recommended_profiles.label: No printer device configuration
intended_for: Systems that do not install printers
avoid_for: Users who add or reconfigure printers

states.rationale: Disable the documented Print Device Configuration Service.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You intentionally do not want this printer setup service running.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You may need printer installation support.
tradeoffs: Unused printer setup plumbing is removed.
Printer installations in progress may be canceled.

However, this review pass did not re-capture a machine-checkable exact Microsoft guidance row for PrintDeviceConfigurationService from the live services page. Under the service-proof methodology, that means the service identity is proven but the official guidance proof is still incomplete, so the record stays in review.

Under the service-proof methodology, local SCM identity proof alone is not enough to restore validated status without exact Microsoft guidance capture.
